flashcards.Back: Determines typical weather characteristics.
It determines typical weather characteristics.
They are determined by atmosphere stability.
Includes radiation, advection, upslope, and steam fog.
This type of fog forms at night.
It typically occurs during the night.
Radiation fog is the type that forms at night.
Forms when moist air moves over a cold surface.
It requires moist air to form.
It requires a cold surface to form.
This condition causes advection fog.
This type of fog is caused by adiabatic cooling.
It results from the process of adiabatic cooling.
This process causes upslope fog.
Forms when cold air moves over warm water.
It requires cold air to form.
It requires warm water to form.
This condition causes steam fog.
Consists of cumulus, mature, and dissipating stages.
The first stage is the cumulus stage.
The second stage is the mature stage.
The third stage is the dissipating stage.
This stage is characterized by updrafts.
It features updrafts.
These occur during the cumulus stage.
This is the stage when precipitation begins.
Precipitation begins during this stage.
It begins in the mature stage.
This stage is characterized by downdrafts.
It features downdrafts.
These occur during the dissipating stage.
A zone between two air masses.
It is the zone between two different air masses.
This is known as a front.
They cause rapid changes in temperature, humidity, and wind.
Rapid temperature changes occur at fronts.
Rapid humidity changes occur at fronts.
Rapid wind changes occur at fronts.
This occurs at the zone between two air masses.
This occurs at the zone between two air masses.
This occurs at the zone between two air masses.
Requires visible water and $0^\circ C$ or colder.
It requires visible water.
It requires a temperature of $0^\circ C$ or colder.
These are the requirements for structural icing.
A format for aviation weather reporting.
A format for aviation weather forecasting.
The METAR format is used for this.
The TAF format is used for this.
Sudden changes in wind speed and/or direction.
